title: Hostess 7 Human Anatomy — Complete Dense Reference
author: Hostess 7 Biology Chamber
disclaimer: Educational anatomy only — not medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

sections.title: Anatomical terminology, planes, regions, cavities
sections.body: Directional terms: superior/cranial (toward head), inferior/caudal (toward feet), anterior/ventral (front), posterior/dorsal (back), medial (toward midline), lateral (away from midline), proximal/distal (limb segments), superficial/deep, ipsilateral/contralateral. Body planes: sagittal (left-right division; midsagittal = midline), frontal/coronal (anterior-posterior), transverse/horizontal (superior-inferior). Regional terms: cephalic, cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral, coccygeal, brachial, antebrachial, carpal, manual, femoral, crural, pedal. Body cavities: dorsal — cranial (brain), vertebral (spinal cord); ventral — thoracic (pleural + pericardial + mediastinum), abdominopelvic (abdominal + pelvic). Serous membranes: parietal vs visceral layers; pleura, pericardium, peritoneum. Quadrants: RUQ, LUQ, RLQ, LLQ. Nine abdominopelvic regions: epigastric, umbilical, hypogastric, hypochondriac L/R, lumbar L/R, iliac L/R.

sections.title: Histology — the four primary tissues
sections.body: Epithelial tissue: covers surfaces, lines cavities, forms glands; avascular; polarity (apical/basal); types — simple squamous (diffusion/filtration), simple cuboidal (secretion/absorption), simple columnar (absorption/secretion; microvilli, goblet cells), pseudostratified columnar (respiratory cilia), stratified squamous (protection; keratinized skin vs nonkeratinized mucosa), transitional (urinary distension). Connective tissue: ECM-rich; fibroblasts, adipocytes, chondrocytes, osteocytes; types — loose areolar (packing), adipose (insulation, energy), dense regular (tendons/ligaments), dense irregular (dermis), elastic, reticular (lymphoid stroma), cartilage (hyaline, elastic, fibrocartilage), bone (compact vs spongy), blood. Muscle tissue: skeletal (striated, voluntary, multinucleated), cardiac (striated, involuntary, intercalated discs), smooth (nonstriated, involuntary, visceral walls). Nervous tissue: neurons (dendrites, soma, axon, synapse) and neuroglia (astrocytes, oligodendrocytes/Schwann cells myelin, microglia, ependymal).

sections.title: Integumentary system — skin, hair, nails, glands
sections.body: Skin layers: epidermis (stratum corneum, lucidum, granulosum, spinosum, basale; keratinocytes, melanocytes, Langerhans, Merkel), dermis (papillary + reticular; collagen, elastin, capillaries, nerves), hypodermis/subcutis (adipose). Functions: barrier, thermoregulation, sensation, vitamin D synthesis, blood reservoir, excretion. Appendages: hair follicle (bulb, root, shaft; arrector pili), nails (nail plate, bed, matrix, lunula). Glands: eccrine sweat (thermoregulation), apocrine (scent; axillary), sebaceous (sebum; holocrine). Wound healing: hemostasis, inflammation, proliferation, remodeling. Burns classified by depth: superficial, partial-thickness, full-thickness.

sections.title: Axial skeleton — skull, vertebral column, thoracic cage
sections.body: Skull: cranial bones — frontal, parietal (2), temporal (2), occipital, sphenoid, ethmoid; facial — maxilla, mandible, zygomatic, nasal, lacrimal, palatine, vomer, inferior nasal conchae. Sutures: coronal, sagittal, lambdoid, squamous. Fontanelles in infancy. Vertebral column (26 in adult): cervical C1 atlas (no body), C2 axis (dens), C3–C7 (C7 vertebra prominens); thoracic T1–T12 (costal facets); lumbar L1–L5 (large bodies); sacrum (5 fused); coccyx (3–5 fused). Curves: cervical lordosis, thoracic kyphosis, lumbar lordosis, sacral kyphosis. Vertebra parts: body, vertebral arch (pedicles, laminae), vertebral foramen, spinous/transverse processes, articular facets. Intervertebral discs: nucleus pulposus, anulus fibrosus. Thoracic cage: sternum (manubrium, body, xiphoid), 12 pairs ribs (true 1–7, false 8–10, floating 11–12), costal cartilages.

sections.title: Appendicular skeleton — pectoral girdle, upper limb, pelvic girdle, lower limb
sections.body: Pectoral girdle: clavicle (S-shaped), scapula (spine, acromion, coracoid, glenoid cavity, supraspinous/infraspinous fossae). Upper limb: humerus (head, greater/lesser tubercles, deltoid tuberosity, medial/lateral epicondyles), radius, ulna (olecranon, trochlear notch), carpals (scaphoid, lunate, triquetrum, pisiform, trapezium, trapezoid, capitate, hamate — 'Some Lovers Try Positions That They Can't Handle'), metacarpals, phalanges. Pelvic girdle: hip bone (ilium, ischium, pubis; acetabulum, obturator foramen), sacroiliac joint. Pelvis: greater/lesser pelvis; pelvic inlet/outlet; male vs female differences (wider outlet, wider subpubic angle in female). Lower limb: femur (head, neck, greater/lesser trochanters, linea aspera, condyles), patella, tibia (medial malleolus), fibula (lateral malleolus), tarsals (calcaneus, talus, navicular, cuboid, cuneiforms), metatarsals, phalanges.

sections.title: Joints — classification, synovial types, major articulations
sections.body: Joint classification: fibrous (sutures, syndesmoses, gomphoses), cartilaginous (synchondroses, symphyses — pubic symphysis, IV discs), synovial (articular cartilage, joint cavity, synovial membrane/fluid, fibrous capsule, reinforcing ligaments). Synovial types: plane/gliding (AC, intercarpal), hinge (elbow, knee, IP joints), pivot (atlantoaxial, radioulnar), condyloid/ellipsoid (radiocarpal, metacarpophalangeal), saddle (carpometacarpal thumb), ball-and-socket (shoulder, hip). Shoulder: glenohumeral — high mobility, shallow glenoid; rotator cuff (SITS: supraspinatus, infraspinatus, teres minor, subscapularis). Knee: tibiofemoral + patellofemoral; cruciate (ACL prevents anterior tibial translation, PCL posterior), collateral (MCL medial, LCL lateral), menisci. Hip: acetabulofemoral — deep socket, weight-bearing stability.

sections.title: Muscles — head, neck, and trunk overview
sections.body: Head/neck muscles: facial expression (orbicularis oculi/oris, zygomaticus, buccinator, frontalis), mastication (masseter, temporalis, medial/lateral pterygoids), neck (sternocleidomastoid — flexion/rotation; trapezius — scapular elevation/depression/retraction; scalenes; platysma). Tongue muscles: intrinsic (shape change) and extrinsic (genioglossus, hyoglossus, styloglossus, palatoglossus). Trunk anterior: rectus abdominis, external/internal obliques, transversus abdominis. Trunk posterior: erector spinae group (iliocostalis, longissimus, spinalis), quadratus lumborum. Thoracic breathing: external intercostals (inspiration), internal intercostals (forced expiration), diaphragm (primary inspiratory muscle — central tendon, costal attachments).

sections.title: Muscles — upper limb and lower limb
sections.body: Upper limb: shoulder — deltoid, supraspinatus; arm — biceps brachii (flexion/supination), triceps (extension); forearm flexors (flexor digitorum superficialis/profundus, flexor carpi radialis/ulnaris), extensors (extensor digitorum, extensor carpi radialis). Hand intrinsic: thenar (opponens pollicis, abductor pollicis brevis), hypothenar, lumbricals, interossei (palmar adduct, dorsal abduct — PAD DAB). Lower limb: gluteal (gluteus maximus/medius/minimus), thigh anterior (quadriceps femoris: rectus femoris, vastus lateralis/medialis/intermedius), posterior (hamstrings: biceps femoris, semitendinosus, semimembranosus), medial (adductor group). Leg: anterior compartment (tibialis anterior, extensor digitorum longus), lateral (fibularis longus/brevis), posterior superficial (gastrocnemius, soleus — calcaneal tendon/Achilles), deep (flexor hallucis longus, flexor digitorum longus, tibialis posterior). Origin/insertion determines action; agonist/antagonist/synergist relationships.

sections.title: Nervous system — CNS, meninges, ventricles, spinal cord
sections.body: CNS: brain and spinal cord. Meninges: dura mater (periosteal + meningeal layers; falx cerebri, tentorium cerebelli), arachnoid mater, pia mater; subdural, subarachnoid spaces; CSF in subarachnoid space. Brain ventricles: lateral (2), third, fourth; choroid plexus produces CSF; flow lateral → III → cerebral aqueduct → IV → central canal/subarachnoid. Spinal cord: cervical enlargement (brachial plexus), lumbar enlargement (lumbosacral plexus), conus medullaris, cauda equina, filum terminale. Gray matter horns (dorsal sensory, ventral motor, lateral sympathetic), white matter columns (ascending sensory tracts, descending motor tracts). Reflex arc: receptor → sensory neuron → integration center → motor neuron → effector.

sections.title: Brain regions — lobes, diencephalon, brainstem, cerebellum
sections.body: Cerebrum lobes: frontal (motor cortex, prefrontal executive function, Broca speech production), parietal (somatosensory cortex, integration), temporal (auditory, Wernicke language comprehension, hippocampus memory), occipital (visual cortex). Diencephalon: thalamus (sensory relay), hypothalamus (homeostasis, autonomic/endocrine link, thirst, hunger, circadian), epithalamus (pineal — melatonin), subthalamus. Basal ganglia: caudate, putamen, globus pallidus, substantia nigra — motor modulation. Limbic: amygdala (emotion/fear), hippocampus. Brainstem: midbrain (superior/inferior colliculi), pons (relay, pneumotaxic center), medulla oblongata (cardiovascular/respiratory centers, decussation of pyramids). Cerebellum: coordination, balance, motor learning — arbor vitae, folia.

sections.title: Cranial nerves I–XII
sections.body: I Olfactory — smell (SVA). II Optic — vision (SA). III Oculomotor — eye movement, pupil constriction, levator palpebrae (GSE/SVE). IV Trochlear — superior oblique (GSE). V Trigeminal — ophthalmic/maxillary/mandibular; sensation face, mastication (GSA/SVE). VI Abducens — lateral rectus (GSE). VII Facial — facial expression, taste anterior 2/3 tongue, lacrimal/submandibular secretion (GSA/SVE). VIII Vestibulocochlear — hearing and balance (SSA). IX Glossopharyngeal — taste posterior 1/3, pharynx sensation, stylopharyngeus, parotid secretion (GSA/SVE). X Vagus — parasympathetic thoracoabdominal viscera, pharynx/larynx muscles, aortic baroreceptors (GSA/SVE). XI Accessory — SCM and trapezius (GSE). XII Hypoglossal — tongue muscles (GSE). Mnemonic functions: Some Say Marry Money But My Brother Says Big Brains Matter More.

sections.title: Special senses — eye, ear, taste, smell
sections.body: Eye: fibrous tunic (cornea transparent, sclera), vascular uvea (choroid, ciliary body, iris), retina (rods dim/night, cones color/fovea centralis). Lens accommodation; aqueous humor (anterior/posterior chambers), vitreous humor. Extraocular muscles: LR6SO4R3 (lateral rectus CN VI, superior oblique CN IV, remaining CN III). Ear: external (pinna, canal, tympanic membrane), middle (ossicles malleus/incus/stapes, Eustachian tube), inner (cochlea hearing, vestibule/semicircular canals balance). Organ of Corti — hair cells, basilar membrane tonotopy. Vestibular apparatus: utricle/saccule (linear acceleration), semicircular canals (angular acceleration). Taste: papillae types; smell: olfactory epithelium, olfactory bulb.

sections.title: Endocrine system — glands and axes
sections.body: Pituitary (hypophysis): anterior (adenohypophysis — GH, TSH, ACTH, FSH, LH, PRL) and posterior (neurohypophysis — ADH, oxytocin release). Hypothalamic-pituitary axes: HPT (TRH→TSH→thyroid T3/T4), HPA (CRH→ACTH→cortisol), HPG (GnRH→FSH/LH→gonadal steroids). Thyroid: isthmus and lobes, parafollicular C cells (calcitonin). Parathyroids (PTH — calcium). Adrenals: cortex (zona glomerulosa aldosterone, fasciculata cortisol, reticularis androgens), medulla (epinephrine/norepinephrine). Pancreatic islets: alpha glucagon, beta insulin, delta somatostatin. Other: pineal melatonin, thymus (T-cell maturation), gonads (estrogen/progesterone, testosterone).

sections.title: Cardiovascular system — heart, circulation, vessels
sections.body: Heart chambers: RA receives systemic venous blood; RV pumps to pulmonary circuit; LA receives oxygenated pulmonary venous blood; LV pumps to systemic aorta. Valves: tricuspid, pulmonary semilunar, mitral/bicuspid, aortic semilunar — prevent backflow. Cardiac cycle: systole (isovolumetric contraction, ejection), diastole (isovolumetric relaxation, ventricular filling); heart sounds S1 (AV close), S2 (semilunar close). Conduction: SA node pacemaker → AV node → bundle of His → Purkinje fibers. Coronary arteries: left main → LAD (anterior), circumflex; right coronary (RCA). Great vessels: superior/inferior vena cava, pulmonary arteries/veins, aorta and branches. Vessel layers: tunica intima, media, externa. Portal system: hepatic portal vein (GI → liver). Pressure gradients drive flow; capillary exchange.

sections.title: Lymphatic and immune anatomy
sections.body: Lymphatic vessels: collect interstitial fluid → lymph nodes → thoracic duct (left subclavian junction) and right lymphatic duct. Primary lymphoid organs: bone marrow (B-cell origin), thymus (T-cell maturation). Secondary: lymph nodes (cortex follicles B, paracortex T, medullary cords/sinuses), spleen (white pulp immune, red pulp filter), MALT (tonsils, Peyer's patches, appendix lymphoid). Spleen functions: blood filtration, immune response, platelet reservoir. Tonsillar ring (Waldeyer). Lymph node regions clinically: cervical, axillary, inguinal, mediastinal, mesenteric.

sections.title: Respiratory system — conducting and respiratory zones
sections.body: Upper: nose/nasal cavity (conchae, meatuses, olfactory), pharynx (nasopharynx, oropharynx, laryngopharynx), larynx (thyroid/cricoid/arytenoid cartilages, true/false vocal folds). Lower: trachea (C-shaped rings), bronchi (primary → lobar → segmental), bronchioles, terminal respiratory bronchioles, alveolar ducts/sacculi/alveoli. Alveolus: type I pneumocytes (gas exchange), type II (surfactant), capillary network. Pleura: visceral on lung, parietal on thoracic wall. Mediastinum divisions. Gas exchange: partial pressure gradients O2 in CO2 out; hemoglobin transport 98.5%, bicarbonate 70% CO2 carriage.

sections.title: Digestive system — alimentary canal and accessory organs
sections.body: Canal: oral cavity (teeth, tongue, salivary glands — parotid, submandibular, sublingual), pharynx, esophagus (upper/lower sphincters), stomach (fundus, body, pylorus; rugae; chief/peptic/G cells), small intestine (duodenum with ampulla of Vater, jejunum, ileum — villi, microvilli), large intestine (cecum/appendix, ascending/transverse/descending/sigmoid colon, rectum, anal canal). Accessory: liver (right/left lobes, caudate/quadrate; lobules; portal triad), gallbladder, pancreas (exocrine acini, endocrine islets; duct of Wirsung). Mesenteries suspend intestines. Greater/lesser omentum. Hepatic portal circulation nutrient processing.

sections.title: Urinary system — kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra
sections.body: Kidney: cortex (glomeruli, convoluted tubules), medulla (pyramids, loops of Henle, collecting ducts), renal pelvis. Nephron: glomerular capsule (Bowman's), PCT, loop of Henle (descending/ascending), DCT, collecting duct. Blood supply: renal artery → segmental → interlobar → arcuate → interlobular → afferent/efferent arterioles → glomerulus. Juxtaglomerular apparatus (RAAS). Ureters (retroperitoneal, trigone entry). Urinary bladder (detrusor muscle, trigone, internal urethral sphincter smooth). Urethra: male (prostatic, membranous, spongy/penile) longer; female shorter — infection risk. Micturition reflex: parasympathetic contraction, somatic external sphincter voluntary control.

sections.title: Male reproductive anatomy
sections.body: Testes (seminiferous tubules, Leydig testosterone, Sertoli support), epididymis (maturation/storage), ductus deferens, ejaculatory ducts, urethra. Accessory: seminal vesicles (fructose fluid), prostate (zones — peripheral most cancer), bulbourethral/Cowper's glands. Penis: root, body, glans; corpora cavernosa/spongiosum; prepuce. Scrotum thermoregulation. Inguinal canal — spermatic cord (vas, vessels, nerves). Blood-testis barrier.

sections.title: Female reproductive anatomy
sections.body: Ovaries (follicles, corpus luteum), uterine tubes/fallopian (fimbriae, ampulla fertilization site), uterus (fundus, body, cervix; endometrium/myometrium/perimetrium), vagina (fornix), external genitalia/vulva (mons pubis, labia majora/minora, clitoris, vestibule, greater vestibular/Bartholin glands). Mammary glands: lobes, lobules, lactiferous ducts/sinuses. Broad ligament suspends uterus. Ovarian and uterine cycles linked via hormones. Pelvic diaphragm support (levator ani, coccygeus).

sections.title: Embryology essentials for anatomy
sections.body: Germ layers: ectoderm (epidermis, nervous system), mesoderm (muscle, bone, cardiovascular, serous linings), endoderm (GI/respiratory epithelium, liver, pancreas). Neurulation: neural plate → tube; somites (sclerotome → vertebrae, myotome → muscle, dermatome → skin sensation). Pharyngeal arches (1 mandibular/maxillary, 2 hyoid, etc.) contribute craniofacial structures. Limb buds: apical ectodermal ridge patterning. Fetal circulation: foramen ovale, ductus arteriosus, umbilical vessels; postnatal closure. Placenta exchange. Teratogenic critical periods.

sections.title: Major arteries, veins, and clinical landmarks
sections.body: Aorta: ascending, arch (brachiocephalic, left CCA, left subclavian), descending thoracic/abdominal (celiac trunk → hepatic/splenic/left gastric; SMA; renal arteries; IMA). Pulse points: carotid, brachial, radial, femoral, popliteal, dorsalis pedis, posterior tibial. Venous: internal/external jugular, subclavian, axillary, basilic/cephalic, median cubital venipuncture, great saphenous. Portal hypertension collateral pathways. Circle of Willis cerebral anastomosis. dural venous sinuses (superior sagittal). Pressure measurement sites correlate with vascular anatomy.

sections.title: Surface anatomy and clinical correlation
sections.body: Landmarks: sternal angle (angle of Louis — T4/5, second rib), nipple line (~T4), umbilicus (L3–L4), anterior superior iliac spine, iliac crest (L4 spinous level). Trachea bifurcation carina (~T5). McBurney's point appendicitis (1/3 from ASIS to umbilicus). Hepatic margin right costal arch. Spleen protected by ribs 9–11. Thoracentesis above rib to avoid neurovascular bundle. Epidural/spinal levels. Dermatomes key levels: C5 deltoid, C6 thumb, C7 middle finger, T4 nipple, T10 umbilicus, L4 knee, S1 little toe. Hesselbach triangle inguinal hernia. Popliteal fossa contents.
